---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 9: Active Learning and Bayesian Optimization Loops
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Active Learning and Bayesian Optimization Loops
- Unit role in closed-loop discovery.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Design data-efficient experiment loops.

## 03. Recap from Unit 8
- Inverse design and optimization context.

## 04. Why active learning in materials
- Expensive labels/experiments.

## 05. Pool-based active learning setup
- Labeled vs unlabeled pools.

## 06. Query strategies overview
- Uncertainty, diversity, expected gain.

## 07. Uncertainty sampling
- Margin/entropy logic.

## 08. Diversity-aware selection
- Avoid redundant queries.

## 09. Expected model change idea
- Influence-driven querying.

## 10. Batch active learning
- Parallel experiment planning.

## 11. Bayesian optimization recap
- Surrogate + acquisition loop.

## 12. Acquisition functions deep dive
- EI, PI, UCB tradeoffs.

## 13. Exploration vs exploitation
- Balancing strategies.

## 14. Noisy observation handling
- Robust updates.

## 15. Constraint-aware BO
- Feasible candidate selection.

## 16. Multi-objective BO
- Pareto-aware acquisition.

## 17. Cost-aware acquisition
- Budget-constrained querying.

## 18. Multi-fidelity querying
- Cheap vs expensive evaluations.

## 19. Human-in-the-loop prioritization
- Expert-informed candidate filters.

## 20. Closed-loop architecture
- Model -> acquisition -> experiment -> update.

## 21. Stopping criteria
- Convergence and value thresholds.

## 22. Regret intuition
- Efficiency performance metric.

## 23. Cold-start challenges
- Initial design strategies.

## 24. Shift-aware querying
- OOD risk control.

## 25. Safety in autonomous loops
- Hard constraints and fail-safes.

## 26. Reproducibility in loop experiments
- Logging every iteration.

## 27. Candidate diversity metrics
- Portfolio quality checks.

## 28. Calibration in BO surrogates
- Reliable uncertainty needed.

## 29. Parallelization strategies
- Async and synchronous loop options.

## 30. Case sketch: alloy optimization loop
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: catalyst screening loop
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: battery composition BO
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Failure mode #1
- Overexploit early surrogate bias.

## 34. Failure mode #2
- Ignore experimental noise.

## 35. Failure mode #3
- Collapse to low-diversity candidates.

## 36. Mitigation checklist
- Robust active-learning guardrails.

## 37. Visualization patterns
- Regret curves, acquisition maps.

## 38. Reporting standards
- Publish loop protocol and seeds.

## 39. Bridge to Unit 10
- Multi-fidelity + physics-informed links.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: simulate AL loop
- Query strategy comparison.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: run BO loop
- Acquisition A/B comparison.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: include constraints
- Feasibility-aware acquisition.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: analyze regret
- Efficiency metrics.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: report outcomes
- Tradeoff and uncertainty discussion.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 46. Summary table
- Methods vs data efficiency.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- Prepare multi-fidelity unit.

## 49. Transition preview
- Unit 10 roadmap.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
